content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check whether a number can be express as sum of two prime numbers.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test contain a single positive integer NNN - the number that needs to be checked.


Output Format

For each test case, print:

`YES` if NNN can be expressed as sum of two primes.
`NO` if not.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if N can be expressed as the sum of two prime numbers, otherwise print NO. Print each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 7 = 2 + 5 (both prime) → YES. 19 = 2 + 17 → YES. 11 cannot be written as sum of two primes → NO.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
YES
NO
NO
YES
NO
YES
YES

solution.approach: Pre‑compute primality of all numbers up to the maximum N among the test cases using the Sieve of Eratosthenes (O(M log log M) time, O(M) memory, where M = max N). For each query N, iterate p from 2 to N/2 and check if both p and N‑p are prime using the pre‑computed table. If such a pair exists, answer YES, otherwise NO. The loop stops early when a valid pair is found, giving an average case much faster than the worst‑case O(N) per query.
solution.timeComplexity: Sieve: O(M log log M) once. For each test case: O(N) in the worst case (checking up to N/2). Total: O(M log log M + Σ N_i).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M) for the boolean prime table.
